title: About NNRG - Institution Overview and Vision
content:
Nalla Narasimha Reddy Education Society's Group of Institutions (NNRG) is a premier multi-disciplinary engineering college established in 2009. The institution is affiliated to JNTU Hyderabad and approved by AICTE (All India Council for Technical Education), New Delhi. NNRG operates an integrated campus housing the School of Engineering, School of Pharmacy, and School of Management Sciences.

NNRG's Vision: To be a premier institution ensuring globally competent and ethically strong professionals.

NNRG's Mission encompasses three key objectives: (1) To provide higher education by refining the traditional methods of teaching to make globally competent professionals; (2) To impart quality education by providing state-of-the-art infrastructure and innovative research facilities; (3) To practice and promote high standards of professional ethics, transparency and accountability.

The institution's core hallmarks are Hard Work, Discipline, and Values and Respect. NNRG's motto is "Dream Big and Aim High."

Key Accreditations and Recognitions:
- Accredited by NAAC (National Assessment and Accreditation Council) with A+ Grade
- NBA (National Board of Accreditation) accreditation for CSE, ECE, and Mechanical Engineering
- Recognized by UGC under section 2(f) of the UGC Act 1956
- ISO 9001:2015 certified
- Conferred autonomous status for 10 years by UGC (November 2021)

Location: NNRG is located at Chowdariguda Village, Ghatkesar Mandal, Ranga Reddy District, Telangana - 500088, approximately 17 km from Secunderabad and 10 km from Uppal Ring Road on Warangal Highway.

title: NNRG Academic Programs - Engineering, Pharmacy and Management
content:
NNRG offers a comprehensive range of undergraduate, postgraduate and research programs across three schools: School of Engineering, School of Pharmacy, and School of Management Sciences.

School of Engineering Programs:
The School of Engineering, established in 2009, offers five B.Tech programs: Computer Science & Engineering (intake 360), Computer Science & Engineering (AI&ML), Computer Science & Engineering (Data Science), Electronics & Communication Engineering (intake 180), Electrical & Electronics Engineering, Mechanical Engineering (intake 120), and Civil Engineering (intake 30). The school also offers M.Tech programs in Computer Science & Engineering, VLSI & Embedded Systems, and Mechanical Engineering (M.Tech intake 18 each).

School of Pharmacy Programs:
Established in 2009, the School of Pharmacy offers B.Pharmacy (4-year program) and M.Pharmacy specializations in Pharmaceutics and Pharmaceutical Analysis. The school has achieved 100% placement rate with graduates employed in national and multinational pharmaceutical companies.

School of Management Sciences:
The MBA program was established in 2009 with an intake of 60 students. The program offers specializations in Finance, Human Resources, and Marketing Management, affiliated to JNTU Hyderabad.

Curriculum and Quality:
NNRG uses industry-endorsed curriculum aligned with JNTU standards and AICTE norms. The institution maintains continuous quality improvement (CQI) processes to evaluate and enhance teaching-learning methods. All programs emphasize outcome-based education (OBE), course outcomes (COs), and program outcomes (POs) alignment.

title: Computer Science & Engineering Department - Overview and Facilities
content:
The Department of Computer Science and Engineering at NNRG was established in 2009 and has grown consistently in strength and facilities. The department is accredited by the National Board of Accreditation (NBA) with accreditation in 2018 and further accreditation in 2022, affirming the quality of its B.Tech program.

Program Details:
The department offers a four-year B.Tech degree in Computer Science and Engineering with an intake of 360 seats, making it one of the largest and most sought-after programs at NNRG.

Faculty and Expertise:
The CSE department is known for its reputed faculty with expertise in diverse fields of computer science and engineering. Faculty members are dedicated to imparting programming knowledge and latest technology innovations to students. The department has professors, associate professors, and assistant professors with strong academic and research backgrounds.

Infrastructure and Laboratories:
CSE boasts seven computer labs with over 400 high-end systems equipped with the latest software recommended by JNTUH and AICTE. The department maintains six dedicated computer centers for undergraduate students and one Elite Research and Development center. All computers in computer centers are connected via LAN. UG and PG students practice all lab sessions in LINUX environment to gain practical exposure.

Industry Collaboration and Student Activities:
The department has collaborated with premier IT industries for providing internships, guest lectures, and workshops on cutting-edge technologies. Students actively participate in co-curricular and extra-curricular activities. Tech Samuuhah is an informal association of CSE students and faculty that conducts various technical events to benefit students in academic and technical skills development.

title: Electronics & Communication Engineering - Program Overview
content:
The Department of Electronics & Communication Engineering was established in 2009 and has emerged as a full-fledged academic department for producing quality ECE engineers. The B.Tech in Electronics and Communication Engineering program is duly approved by AICTE and affiliated to JNTU Hyderabad with an intake of 180 seats.

Academic Programs:
The ECE department offers B.Tech with 180 seats and an M.Tech program with specialization in VLSI & Embedded System Engineering started in 2013 with an intake of 18 seats. The department is accredited by the National Board of Accreditation (NBA) in the year 2018.

Faculty Credentials:
All faculty members have a minimum of 4 years of teaching experience. The department is led by Dr. Ravi Bolimera as Head of Department. Faculty expertise spans microelectronics, mobile communications, VLSI design automation, signal processing, and communication systems.

Infrastructure and Research Focus:
The department has spacious laboratories and state-of-the-art equipment for various specialized labs. Regular seminars, guest lectures, workshops, and technical symposia are conducted to keep faculty and students updated with the latest developments in various technologies.

Program Outcomes:
The department is committed to providing students the opportunity to embark on a rewarding and exciting learning journey in preparation for a future career in electronics and communication engineering. Students gain expertise ranging from microelectronics to VLSI design automation through classroom instruction and hands-on laboratory experience.

title: Mechanical Engineering Department - Specializations and Labs
content:
The Mechanical Engineering department at NNRG was established in 2009 with a mission to offer quality education to aspiring mechanical engineers. The department initially had an intake of 60 students which increased to 120 in 2012 due to overwhelming response. The department is accredited by the National Board of Accreditation (NBA) in 2018.

Academic Programs:
The department offers a four-year B.Tech in Mechanical Engineering with an intake of 120 seats and a two-year M.Tech in Mechanical Engineering with an intake of 18 seats.

Faculty and Teaching:
The responsibility of educating students is efficiently handled by highly qualified faculty members with phenomenal expertise in all mechanical engineering specializations. Faculty members clearly explain concepts and provide competent classroom coaching that plays an important role in student academic performance and career development.

Laboratory Infrastructure:
The department features state-of-the-art laboratories enabling students to participate effectively in practical sessions. Advanced lab equipment allows students to conduct various experiments and research activities. The CAD laboratory is particularly significant, featuring design software like Pro-E and AutoCAD, along with simulation software like ANSYS. These tools are essential for research work and academic projects. The department also features a CNC machine, typically used in industries, providing students with practical training on actual industrial equipment.

Industry Readiness:
NNRG's location near industrial hubs enables close proximity to mechanical engineering companies. The department leverages this advantage by conducting practical training aligned with industry requirements. This approach has led to astounding placement results, with graduates well-prepared to tackle challenging job roles after completing their studies.

title: NNRG Central Library - Collections and Services
content:
The NNRG Central Library is a state-of-the-art facility designed to provide comprehensive support for teaching, learning, and research activities. The library is fully computerized using KOHA Integrated Library Management Software Package with Online Public Access Catalogue (OPAC) facility accessible through intranet.

Library Collections:
The library is spread over a floor area of approximately 1,400 square meters and maintains more than 30,000 volumes of books covering about 5,000 titles. Collections include textbooks, reference books, journals, magazines, and e-journals from various organizations. The library is an institutional member of DELNET.

Resource Categories:
- Books: 30,000+ volumes (5,000+ titles)
- CD/DVDs: 500
- Project Reports: 400
- Print Journals: 228 in various engineering, management, and pharmacy disciplines
- Online E-Journals: Approximately 600 accessed through DELNET subscription

Library Services and Timings:
The library operates from 8:30 AM to 5:00 PM on all working days. Services include: (1) Lending Service with open access system; (2) Periodical Section with national/international print journals; (3) Reference Section with reading hall and specialized collections; (4) Digital Library with 32 systems, LAN connection, and 10 Mbps bandwidth; (5) Reprographic Services including photocopying at ₹1 per copy.

Borrowing Privileges:
UG Students: 3 books for 15 days (₹1/day late fee)
PG Students: 3 books for 15 days (₹1/day late fee)
Teaching Staff: 4 books for one month
Non-Teaching Staff: 2 books for one month

Library Staff:
- K. Srinivas - Librarian
- B. Umamaheswara Rao - Assistant Librarian
- Additional library assistants and attendants support operations

title: NNRG Transportation Services - Routes and Schedules
content:
NNRG provides comprehensive transportation services through a large fleet of buses covering multiple routes across Hyderabad. The campus is strategically located at about 17 km from Secunderabad/Koti and 10 km from Uppal Ring Road on Warangal Highway, making transportation essential for students.

Transportation Fleet:
A huge fleet of buses operates from almost all corners of the city with well-trained and experienced drivers. The buses are modern and comfortable, designed for student safety and convenience.

Transportation Fees:
Transportation fees range from ₹20,000 to ₹30,000 depending on the distance traveled. Students can select routes based on their residential area and proximity.

Bus Routes Overview:
NNRG operates 12 dedicated bus routes, each with a dedicated driver and vehicle. Most routes converge at the campus around 8:45 AM to ensure timely arrival for classes.

Sample Route Details:
Route 1: Covers Miyapur, JNTU, KPHB, Kukatpally, and surrounding areas; Driver: Narendra Kumar (93909 43533)
Route 2: Covers Moosapet, Erragadda, SR Nagar areas; Driver: G Yadagiri (9398678241)
Route 3: Covers Padma Rao Nagar, Chilkalguda, Amberpet areas; Driver: V Tirupathi (99490 39544)

RTC Services:
The RTC (Road Transport Corporation) also runs city buses at good frequency towards Ghatkesar, Korremula, and Narapally for additional connectivity options.

title: NNRG Girls Hostel - Facilities and Accommodations
content:
NNRG provides on-campus hostel accommodation for outstation female students in a secure and supportive environment. The girls hostel is an integral part of the campus infrastructure, offering a home-like atmosphere with comprehensive facilities.

Hostel Warden:
A. Vijaya Laxmi is the dedicated hostel warden who oversees all hostel operations and student welfare.

Safety and Security:
The hostel maintains 24/7 security vigilance with round-the-clock emergency care services. The institution provides girls with enhanced safety measures including a dedicated vehicle exclusively for hostel students to transport them to the hospital if they fall sick or require medical attention. The hostel environment emphasizes hygiene and safety, maintaining a ragging-free campus as per UGC norms.

Facilities and Amenities:
- 24/7 medical care facility with on-call medical support
- Spacious rooms with comfortable bedding
- Dedicated study rooms for academic work
- Indoor and outdoor games facilities
- Entertainment hall with multi-channel television facility
- Exclusive visitors lounge for receiving guests
- Treated RO water in all floors for drinking purpose
- Solar heaters for hot water supply
- Clean and hygienic environment throughout
- Fine infrastructure and exemplary facilities

Hostel Culture:
The hostel is designed to create a feeling of togetherness among residents while maintaining individual comfort. The combination of security, medical care, recreational facilities, and academic support makes NNRG hostel a second home for students.

title: NNRG Admissions Process - Application and Eligibility
content:
NNRG offers admissions across multiple programs through both merit-based (state counseling) and management quota pathways. Students can pursue B.Tech, B.Pharmacy, M.Tech, M.Pharmacy, and MBA programs.

Admission Pathways:
1. Merit-based Admission: Through JNTUH state counseling based on EAMCET/JEE rankings
2. Management Quota: Category-B, NRI, and NRI-Sponsored seats available for B.Tech and B.Pharmacy
3. Academic Performance-based: For lateral entry and postgraduate programs

Application Process:
For regular admissions, students participate in state-level counseling after qualifying entrance exams. For management quota, applications are submitted online through the NNRG portal.

Management Quota Application:
Students can apply for Management Category-B seats online at https://nnrg.edu.in/bcategory/index.php. The application requires details including 10th/Intermediate marks, EAMCET/JEE scores (if applicable), and preferred courses/branches.

Required Documents:
- SSC and Intermediate mark sheets or equivalent
- EAMCET/JEE hall ticket and score sheet
- Community certificate (if applicable)
- Aadhaar card and ID proofs
- Address proof
- Passport size photographs

Eligibility Criteria:
- B.Tech: Passed Intermediate (10+2) or equivalent with qualifying EAMCET/JEE score
- B.Pharmacy: Similar eligibility with EAMCET/JEE scores
- M.Tech: B.Tech degree with qualifying GATE score or JNTUH entrance exam
- MBA: Any graduate with qualifying entrance exam score or CAT/GMAT

Admissions Contact:
Phone: 040 29705282
Email: adminssions@nnrg.edu.in

title: NNRG Placements and Recruitment - Statistics and Process
content:
NNRG maintains a strong placement record through its dedicated Training & Placement (T&P) cell that facilitates campus recruitment drives and industry interactions. The institution has a comprehensive placement infrastructure supporting student career development.

Major Recruiters:
NNRG attracts recruitment drives from leading national and multinational companies including:
- IT Companies: TCS, Infosys, Wipro, GENPACT, HCL, DeltaX
- Banking/Finance: ICICI Bank, Karvy Stock Broking, ADP
- Pharma Companies: Divi's Laboratories, Hetero Pharma, Aurobindo, Astra Microwave
- Other sectors: Sutherland, Medfile, PIE INFOCOMM, BYJU'S, 10000 Coders

Placement Highlights:
Recent placement achievements include 67 students placed at BYJU'S with ₹7.0 LPA package, multiple TCS placements for 2026 batch (including TCS Digital and TCS NINJA offers), and ICICI Bank MBA recruitment drives.

Internship and Training Programs:
The college partners with leading organizations including IBM, Infosys Foundation, and ICT Academy for internship placements and skill development. Campus Recruitment Training (CRT) is conducted starting from 2nd semester for students to develop aptitude and interview skills.

Placement Process:
1. Campus Recruitment Training (CRT) for skill development
2. Industry guest lectures and workshops
3. Resume building and interview preparation
4. Pool campus drives and on-campus recruitment drives
5. Internship placements during 3rd and 4th years
6. Final placement in 4th year

School-wise Performance:
School of Pharmacy: 100% placement rate with graduates employed in pharmaceutical firms
School of Engineering: Regular placements with package offerings from ₹4.5 LPA to ₹7+ LPA
School of Management Sciences: Placement in companies like Tech Mahindra, Genpact, ADP

title: NNRG Events and Activities - Technical and Cultural Programs
content:
NNRG hosts a vibrant calendar of technical, cultural, and co-curricular events throughout the academic year, providing students with exposure to industry practices and personal development opportunities.

Major Technical Symposiums:
Tech Samprathi: A two-day national-level technical symposium organized by CSE department featuring competitions and technical talks.
Pharmasamprathi: A three-day national-level technical symposium organized by School of Pharmacy for students across pharmaceutical disciplines.
RTIME (Recent Trends & Innovations in Mechanical Engineering): National conference organized by Mechanical department showcasing innovations and research.
RAECE (Recent Advances in Electronics & Communication Engineering): National conference organized by ECE department.

Management and Cultural Events:
PARAMPARA: A national-level management fest organized by the School of Management Sciences featuring business plan competitions, case studies, and leadership activities.
MUN (Model United Nations): An annual event where students simulate United Nations proceedings and diplomatic roles.
Hackathons: Industry Hackathons and internal hackathons (SIH - Smart India Hackathon) for innovation and problem-solving.

Faculty Development Programs:
AICTE-sponsored ATAL (Approved Teaching Learning) Faculty Development Programs on emerging topics like AI, IoT, VLSI, and signal processing.
ISTE-approved one-week refresher programs on pedagogy and technical topics.
Workshops on industry-relevant technologies through ICT Academy partnerships.

Student Development Initiatives:
5-day workshops on emerging technologies (IoT, UI/UX Design, Data Analytics, Python, Cyber Security)
Guest lectures from industry experts and senior academics
Industrial visits to leading companies like INFOSYS, NRSC, and other IT/Manufacturing firms
Skill development programs on campus corporate training

Club Activities:
Tech Samuuhah (CSE club), Pharmacy Club, IETE Student Forum, IEEE Student Chapter, and various professional and cultural societies conduct regular activities and competitions.
